activities_projects.title: The Three Dimensions of Food Security
activities_projects.objective: Understand the basic components of being food secure
steps: Define 'Availability', 'Accessibility', and 'Affordability' in simple terms
Give an example of 'Accessibility' (e.g., Is there a shop nearby?)
Explain what happens to 'Affordability' when prices of grains rise suddenly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the three essential requirements for food security
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct definitions and relevant examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Impact of Natural Calamities
activities_projects.objective: Learn how disasters like drought affect food supply
steps: Read the section on the '1943 Bengal Famine' in the textbook
List the steps of how a total lack of rain leads to starvation (Crop failure -> Shortage -> Price rise -> Famine)
Identify who are the most affected people during such a calamity
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the link between environmental factors and food shortage
activities_projects.evaluation: Clear flow of the 'starvation' process and identification of victims (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chronic vs. Seasonal Hunger
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between the two types of hunger
steps: Describe 'Seasonal Hunger' using the example of a casual construction worker
Describe 'Chronic Hunger' as a result of consistently low income
Make a small table showing the differences between the two
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between types of hunger based on duration and cause

activities_projects.title: The Role of Buffer Stock
activities_projects.objective: Understand how the government prepares for emergencies
steps: Define 'Buffer Stock' as used by the Food Corporation of India (FCI)
Draw a simple diagram of a granary/silo where grains are stored
Explain why storing extra grain is important for a country like India
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns about the government's strategy for food storage
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct definition and logical reasoning for storage (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Minimum Support Price (MSP) Awareness
activities_projects.objective: Understand how the government supports farmers
steps: Define MSP (Minimum Support Price) as the price announced before the sowing season
Explain how MSP encourages farmers to grow more wheat and rice
Note who buys the grain from farmers at this price (FCI)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes MSP as an incentive for agricultural production
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate definition of MSP and its purpose (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Visiting a Ration Shop (PDS)
activities_projects.objective: Observe the Public Distribution System in action
steps: Visit a local Fair Price Shop (Ration Shop)
List the items sold there (e.g., Rice, Wheat, Sugar, Kerosene)
Observe or find out the 'Issue Price' and compare it to the market price
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects theoretical knowledge of PDS to local reality
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed list of items and price comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ration Cards and Categories
activities_projects.objective: Identify different cards for different economic groups
steps: Identify the three types of cards: Antyodaya (poorest), BPL (Below Poverty Line), and APL
Assign the correct card to a family based on their income level
Explain the 'Antyodaya Anna Yojana' scheme briefly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the targeting of food aid in India
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct categorization of cards and schemes (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Government Food Schemes Checklist
activities_projects.objective: Learn about other food-related welfare programs
steps: Explain the 'Integrated Child Development Services' (ICDS)
Describe the 'Mid-day Meal' scheme in schools
Write one way these schemes help improve the health of children
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies diverse ways the government ensures nutrition
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct explanation of ICDS and Mid-day Meals (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Role of Cooperatives
activities_projects.objective: Understand non-government efforts in food security
steps: Identify a famous cooperative like 'Amul' (Gujarat) or 'Mother Dairy' (Delhi)
Describe how they provide milk and vegetables at controlled rates
Note the role of 'Grain Banks' started by NGOs in Maharashtra
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the contribution of cooperatives to food security
activities_projects.evaluation: Relevant examples and description of their impact (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Self-Sufficiency in Grains
activities_projects.objective: Celebrate India's progress since the Green Revolution
steps: Recall the 'Green Revolution' and the introduction of HYV seeds
Explain how India avoided famines even during adverse weather after the 1970s
Define 'Self-sufficiency' in the context of food grains
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Appreciates the impact of agricultural modernization on food security
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical explanation of India's self-sufficiency (10 marks)
